### Monitoring Narrative:
Training monitoring data — past 28 days review: Athlete profile: recreational Rugby Union Blindside Flanker, training age 2 years, phase: in season early. The acute:chronic ratio (0.906) is in the sweet spot, indicating appropriate progressive loading. Critical: HRV at 30.2ms — 20.1ms below individual baseline for 7 consecutive mornings. HRV trend is stable. Sleep duration slightly reduced at 6.9 hours per night. The athlete is reporting below-normal wellness in the following areas: sleep quality, fatigue, muscle soreness, mood, stress. Composite status: moderately depressed.

### Audience: athlete

### Load Management Brief:
YOUR STATUS: HIGH

The combination of signals present indicates a high-risk state.

TRAINING LOAD:
Your training load is well-balanced (0.91 ratio). Good work.

PHYSIOLOGICAL READINESS (HRV):
Your recovery score has been very low for 7 days in a row — this is a clear signal to rest.

SUBJECTIVE WELLNESS:
Multiple wellness areas are flagged: sleep quality and fatigue and muscle soreness. Overall you are feeling moderately depressed.

SIGNAL INTEGRATION:
Monitoring signals show a notable discrepancy: Training load is within acceptable range but HRV is suppressed and wellness is depressed — a non-training stressor is likely responsible. This pattern suggests non training stressor likely. Recommended interpretation: investigate external stressors.

CURRENT STATUS:
Your body is showing overtraining warning signs that need 2-6 weeks of reduced training.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
• IF if non-training stressor identified: maintain training load, address the stressor directly — signal to watch: HRV and wellness should respond within 48-72 hours
• IF if no stressor identified after investigation: reduce load 20% as precaution — signal to watch: reassess in 3 days — if no improvement, seek medical review
• Athlete conversation recommended before any load modification — explore sleep, nutrition, life stress, and illness.

ESCALATION TRIGGERS:
Talk to your coach or doctor if things do not improve after a few easy days.

Additionally, Critical: HRV at 48.0ms — 10.5ms below individual baseline for 7 consecutive mornings. Context: athlete returning after 6-day general viral enforced rest period.
Wellness is good across all dimensions — no subjective readiness concerns.

Monitoring signals show a notable discrepancy: Objective HRV indicates physiological fatigue but the athlete is reporting good subjective state — subclinical fatigue the athlete has not yet noticed.
